data.title: Beam Global: Strong Q4 Outperformance, Improving Margins, and Undervalued Growth Support Buy Rating and $3.50 Target
data.description:
Tate Sullivan, an analyst from Maxim Group, maintained the Buy rating on Beam Global. The associated price target is $3.50.

Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Tate Sullivan has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors including stronger-than-expected quarterly results and improving profitability trends. Beam Global’s preliminary fourth-quarter revenue and margins significantly outpaced prior forecasts, with revenue rising sharply quarter over quarter and gross margin turning positive and expanding, indicating better pricing power and operational efficiency.

Sullivan also highlights the company’s growing mix of higher-margin commercial and international customers, which is expected to support substantial revenue growth and margin expansion through 2026. Despite ongoing EBITDA losses and modest cash levels, he believes Beam can fund operations via existing cash, at-the-market equity issuance, and available debt facilities, and notes that the current valuation multiple remains low relative to projected sales, supporting a Buy rating and a $3.50 price target.

Sullivan covers the Industrials sector, focusing on stocks such as Seanergy Maritime, Performance Shipping, and Stardust Power. According to TipRanks, Sullivan has an average return of 3.1% and a 49.50% success rate on recommended stocks.

data.title: Barfresh: Arps Acquisition, Education Channel Expansion, and Strengthened Balance Sheet Underpin Buy Rating and $6 Target
data.description:
Maxim Group analyst Anthony Vendetti maintained a Buy rating on Barfresh Food Group on April 2 and set a price target of $6.00.

Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Anthony Vendetti has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Barfresh’s growth prospects and improving operational profile. He highlights that the Arps Dairy acquisition has driven record revenue and enabled the company to bring the vast majority of its production in-house, which should improve supply chain control and operating efficiency over time, despite near-term margin pressure.

Vendetti also points to the new long-term school district contract and the still-low penetration in the education channel as indicators of substantial room for further expansion. In addition, he views the recent financing and government grant as sufficient to fund construction of the new Ohio facility and support operations until cash flow turns positive, reinforcing confidence in his multi‑year forecasts. Finally, he sees the current valuation as compelling relative to peers, with a justified premium based on expected above‑average revenue growth and the strategic benefits of the Arps acquisition, supporting his Buy recommendation and $6.00 price target.

BRFH’s price has also changed moderately for the past six months – from $3.200 to $2.800, which is a -12.50% drop .
